required_source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

source_classes.class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
source_classes.rationale: FAA, DoD, and NIST guidance provides baseline expectations for manufacturing controls, production accountability, and quality context in aerospace continuity workflows.

source_classes.class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
source_classes.rationale: IAQG SCMH and DoD materiail management guidance define supplier, lot, and inventory coordination mechanics and handoff workflows.

source_classes.class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
source_classes.rationale: FAA Part 21, DFARS, and NIST supply-chain risk references define release and hold conditions this lane cannot bypass.

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 4140.01, DoD Supply Chain Materiel Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary defense materiel-management directive that defines planning, readiness reporting, and supply ownership expectations for mission material flow.

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.246-7007, Counterfeit Electronic Part Detection and Avoidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative clause-level requirement for electronic part source control and evidence handling in defense procurement-related supply chains.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Supplier eligibility and risk boundary checks

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.246-7008, Sources of Electronic Parts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official clause defining accepted source documentation and supplier chain constraints for defense electronic components.

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 21 - Certification Procedures for Products and Articles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Aviation production, change, and release boundaries that govern whether coordination may proceed versus require release-control owners.

authoritative_sources.title: FAA AC 21-43A, Production Under 14 CFR Part 21
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA public operating guidance for aerospace production and supplier controls that define evidence expectations before coordination completion.

authoritative_sources.title: 9100 Quality Management Systems - Requirements for Aviation, Space and Defense Organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry-standard framework that defines quality language and supplier quality context for aerospace supply continuity.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Terminology mapping and handoff artifact normalization

authoritative_sources.title: IAQG Supply Chain Management Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized aerospace supply-chain workflow reference for lot and supplier interface controls.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-161 Rev. 1, Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Framework for classifying supplier risk and dependency impact used for escalation and risk communication in shortage scenarios.

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture request intent, mission criticality, and required tenant records before any owner routing or closure claim.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and traceability assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect ERP/MES/WMS/Supplier lot, hold, and movement evidence and identify conflicts.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Ownership and blocker mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map dependencies across planning, inventory, logistics, quality, supplier, and release owners; mark duplicates and missing links.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Escalation and handoff execution
workflow_stages.description: Create routing, shortage escalation, and closure outputs only when authority and evidence prerequisites are complete.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Tenant-policy convergence
workflow_stages.description: Apply tenant policy overrides; escalate when public guidance and tenant doctrine materially conflict and cannot be harmonized.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

allowed: Coordinate supply continuity, inventory status, shortage risk, and routing dependencies with explicit artifacts and owner mapping.
Issue blocker logs and handoff outputs where prerequisites and authority checks are complete.
Escalate supplier substitution, release-boundary, safety-critical, and legal or financial cases to adjacent owners.
escalate: Missing tenant authority mapping would change routing
Conflicting evidence across source-of-truth systems remains unresolved
Public and tenant policy diverge on hold or release boundaries
refuse: Request asks for PO execution, reroute freight, hold release, or procurement/quality final authorization.
Request asks for supplier substitution approval without supplier/engineering/quality authority.
Completion is requested before evidence or acceptance requirements are met.

domain_failure_modes: Treating aerospace shortage coordination as generic procurement action and routing execution directly instead of escalating to owners.
Ignoring lot-traceability conflicts by allowing confident handoff despite contradictory ERP/MES/WMS records.
Skipping quality hold/release checks and producing completion or closure outputs under unresolved evidence.
Overriding supplier-substitution and release boundaries due to urgency without escalation.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
Lane is asked to absorb execution work
Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Normal in-scope execution with industry terminology

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different programs may define mission-essential outcomes by different risk and tolerance criteria.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require explicit tenant criticality policy at intake and block completion when absent.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Supplier approval flow and waiver chain
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Some organizations require dual approval for approved source deviations; some require legal review.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Route supplier deviations to the correct adjacent chain and log required owners and missing confirmations.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different tenants define stale vs live inventory differently by mission posture.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use tenant-defined freshness policy as a required input and avoid completion if unspecified.

notes: Research covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling dimensions required for this lane.
All required source classes are included and each is tied to at least one required workflow stage.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; each open point is explicitly represented as a required tenant-policy or confirmation condition.
